Name: ObservationInterpretationCodes
Title: Observation Interpretation Codes
Description: A categorical assessment, providing a rough qualitative interpretation of the observation value,    such as “normal”/ “abnormal”,”low” / “high”, “better” / “worse”, “susceptible” / “resistant”, “expected”/ “not expected”.    The value set is intended to be for ANY use where coded representation of an interpretation is needed.             Notes:          This is being communicated in v2.x in OBX-8 (Observation Interpretation), in v3 in ObservationInterpretation (CWE) in R1 (Representative Realm) and in FHIR in    Observation.interpretation. Historically these values come from the laboratory domain, and these codes are extensively    used. The value set binding is extensible, so codes outside the value set that are needed for interpretation concepts    (i.e. particular meanings) that are not included in the value set can be used, and these new codes may also be added to    the value set and published in a future version.

Name: ActCode
Title: v3 Code System ActCode
Description: A code specifying the particular kind of Act that the Act-instance represents within its class.  Constraints:  The kind of Act (e.g. physical examination, serum potassium, inpatient encounter, charge financial transaction, etc.) is specified with a code from one of several, typically external, coding systems.  The coding system will depend on the class of Act, such as LOINC for observations, etc. Conceptually, the Act.code must be a specialization of the Act.classCode. This is why the structure of ActClass domain should be reflected in the superstructure of the ActCode domain and then individual codes or externally referenced vocabularies subordinated under these domains that reflect the ActClass structure. Act.classCode and Act.code are not modifiers of each other but the Act.code concept should really imply the Act.classCode concept. For a negative example, it is not appropriate to use an Act.code "potassium" together with and Act.classCode for "laboratory observation" to somehow mean "potassium laboratory observation" and then use the same Act.code for "potassium" together with Act.classCode for "medication" to mean "substitution of potassium". This mutually modifying use of Act.code and Act.classCode is not permitted.

In definition mood it indicates the available priorities.

That is, whether a person identifies as being of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander origin. The values are those concepts defined in the Australian Indigenous Status code system (https://healthterminologies.gov.au/fhir/CodeSystem/australian-indigenous-status-1).

Name: AustralianMedication
Title: Australian Medication
Description: The Australian Medication value set includes all Australian Medicines Terminology (AMT) product concepts that may be used for the identification of a medicine, vaccine or other therapeutic good. This is suitable for the recording of a product which may be ordered for, administered to, or used by an individual.

Name: AustralianMedicationUse
Title: Australian Medication Use
Description: The Australian Medication Use value set extends the NCTS Australian Medication value set by including all Australian Medicines Terminology (AMT) product concepts that may be used for the identification of a medicine, vaccine or other therapeutic good, together with the concept 787481004 |No known medications| (SNOMED CT). This value set is suitable for recording the medication or asserting the absence of known medications in a Medication Statement.

Name: AustralianStatesAndTerritories
Title: Australian States and Territories
Description: The Australian States and Territories value set includes values that represent the Australian states and territories.

Name: AustralianVeteranStatus
Title: Australian Veteran Status
Description: The Australian Veteran Status value set includes values to support the representation of a person's veteran status in the Australian Defence Force. The values are those concepts defined in the Australian Veteran Status code system (https://healthterminologies.gov.au/fhir/CodeSystem/australian-veteran-status-1).

Name: BodySite
Title: Body Site
Description: The Body Site value set includes values that cover a broad range of anatomical and acquired body structures suitable for identifying a body site.

Name: BodySiteRelativeSiteQualifier
Title: Body Site Relative Site Qualifier
Description: The Body Site Relative Site Qualifier value set includes values that may be used to represent a relative body site when qualifying an anatomical location.

Name: ClinicalCondition
Title: Clinical Condition
Description: The Clinical Condition value set includes values that cover a broad range of clinical concepts to support the representation of conditions, including problems, diagnoses and disorders.

Name: ClinicalFinding
Title: Clinical Finding
Description: The Clinical Finding value set includes a broad range of values that support the recording of disorders and clinical findings, including observations, judgments or assessments about subjects of care.

Name: ClinicalSpecialty
Title: Clinical Specialty
Description: The Clinical Specialty value set includes values that may be used to describe a clinical specialty performed by an entity.

Name: CommonLanguagesInAustralia
Title: Common Languages in Australia
Description: The Common Languages in Australia value set includes languages that are commonly encountered in Australia, including a comprehensive set of Australian Indigenous languages.

Name: ConcurrentSupplyGrounds
Title: Concurrent Supply Grounds
Description: The Concurrent Supply Grounds value set includes values that may be used for the grounds that authorise a subsidy for the concurrent supply of an item specified in a prescription and all of its repeats.

Name: ContactRelationshipType
Title: Contact Relationship Type
Description: The Contact Relationship Type value set includes values that may be used to describe the nature of the relationship between the subject and the contact person.

Name: DateAccuracyIndicator
Title: Date Accuracy Indicator
Description: The Date Accuracy Indicator value set includes values that indicate the accuracy of components of a reported date.

Name: EncounterType
Title: Encounter Type
Description: The Encounter Type value set includes values that can be used to represent types of healthcare interactions.

Name: EvaluationProcedure
Title: Evaluation Procedure
Description: The Evaluation Procedure value set includes values that cover a broad range of procedures including specialist investigations, imaging procedures, pathology procedures, and other diagnostic investigations.

Name: HealthcareOrganisationRoleType
Title: Healthcare Organisation Role Type
Description: The Healthcare Organisation Role Type value set includes values that can represent the role, use or function of a healthcare related organisation or facility.

Name: IndividualHealthcareIdentifierRecordStatus
Title: Individual Healthcare Identifier Record Status
Description: The Individual Healthcare Identifier Record Status value set includes values that may be used to describe the classification status of a record of an Individual Healthcare Identifier.

Name: IndividualHealthcareIdentifierStatus
Title: Individual Healthcare Identifier Status
Description: The Individual Healthcare Identifier Status value set includes values that may be used to describe the classification status of an Individual Healthcare Identifier.

Name: ImagingAnatomicRegionOfInterest
Title: Imaging Anatomic Region Of Interest
Description: The Imaging Anatomic Region of Interest value set includes values that may be used to represent the anatomic area of interest of a diagnostic imaging procedure.

Name: ImagingProcedure
Title: Imaging Procedure
Description: The Imaging Procedure value set includes values that support the representation of medical imaging procedures.

Name: ImmunisationAnatomicalSite
Title: Immunisation Anatomical Site
Description: The Immunisation Anatomical Site value set includes values that can be used to describe the anatomical site where an immunisation may be administered.

Name: ImmunisationRouteOfAdministration
Title: Immunisation Route of Administration
Description: The Immunisation Route of Administration value set includes values that can be used to describe the route by which a vaccine is administered.

Name: IndicatorOfHypersensitivityOrIntoleranceToSubstance
Title: Indicator of Hypersensitivity or Intolerance to Substance
Description: The Indicator of Hypersensitivity or Intolerance to Substance value set includes values that may be used to indicate or state a propensity to adverse reaction to a substance. The values represent a range of concept types such as substances, medicines, clinical findings and negated allergy findings.

Name: MedicationForm
Title: Medication Form
Description: The Medication Form value set includes values that represent the dose form of a medication.

Name: MedicationIngredient
Title: Medication Ingredient
Description: The Medication Ingredient value set includes values that may be used to identify a substance or a medicinal product that is an ingredient of a medication.

Name: MedicationReasonTaken
Title: Medication Reason Taken
Description: The Medication Reason Taken value set includes values that identify a reason why a medication has been or is being taken.

Name: MedicineSubstitutionReason
Title: Medicine Substitution Reason
Description: The Medicine Substitution Reason value set includes values that may be used to record the reason for medicine substitution.

Name: PathologyDiagnosticServiceCategory
Title: Pathology Diagnostic Service Category
Description: The Pathology Diagnostic Service Category value set includes values that may be used to indicate the diagnostic service where a pathology observation was performed.

Name: PractitionerRole
Title: Practitioner Role
Description: The Practitioner Role value set includes values that describe the role that a practitioner is authorised to perform within an organisation.

Name: Procedure
Title: Procedure
Description: The Procedure value set includes values that cover a broad range of procedures and clinical interventions that can be associated with a subject of care.

Name: ReasonForEncounter
Title: Reason For Encounter
Description: The Reason For Encounter value set includes values that can be used to represent the reason an encounter takes place.

Name: ReasonForRequest
Title: Reason for Request
Description: The Reason for Request value set includes values that can be used to indicate the rationale for a request. For example, this may include the reason for requesting a service or diagnostic procedure.

Name: ReasonVaccineAdministered
Title: Reason Vaccine Administered
Description: The Reason Vaccine Administered value set includes values that describe why a vaccine may be administered.

Name: ReasonVaccineNotAdministered
Title: Reason Vaccine Not Administered
Description: The Reason Vaccine Not Administered value set includes values that describe why a vaccine was not administered.

Name: RelatedPersonRelationshipType
Title: Related Person Relationship Type
Description: The Related Person Relationship Type value set includes values that may be used to describe the nature of the relationship between the subject and the related person.

Name: RouteOfAdministration
Title: Route of Administration
Description: The Route of Administration value set includes values that describe the route by which a medicine may be administered.

Name: SeparationMode
Title: Separation Mode
Description: The Separation Mode value set includes values that can be used to indicate the category of discharge of a patient. Separation refers to the process by which an episode of care for an admitted patient ceases.

Name: ServiceType
Title: Service Type
Description: The Service Type value set includes values that describe a broad range of service types.

Name: SpecimenCollectionProcedure
Title: Specimen Collection Procedure
Description: The Specimen Collection Procedure value set includes values to represent the method by which a specimen was collected.

Name: SpecimenType
Title: Specimen Type
Description: The Specimen Type value set includes values to represent a specimen collected or tested in a pathological investigation.

Name: RCPASPIAPathologyReporting
Title: RCPA SPIA Pathology Reporting
Description: The RCPA SPIA Pathology Reporting value set includes standard codes for use in reporting chemical pathology, haematology, immunopathology and microbiology serology results in Australia. The values represent the observables against which results are reported. It combines several Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia (RCPA) Standards for Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) value sets. The composition of this value set, in referencing other value sets, does not transiently include the designations and displays defined in the referenced value sets.

Name: Substance
Title: Substance
Description: The Substance value set includes values that support the representation of a broad range of substances. Included are the broadest range of substance values typically available in SNOMED CT along with the medicinal substances from the Australian Medicines Terminology.

Name: VaccinationTargetDisease
Title: Vaccination Target Disease
Description: The Vaccination Target Disease value set includes values that can represent vaccine preventable diseases. The content coverage includes diseases caused by a microorganism or bacterial toxin.

Name: spia-chemical-pathology-refset
Title: RCPA - SPIA Chemical Pathology Value Set
Description: Standard codes for use in reporting chemical pathology results in Australia. Content is derived from the RCPA Standardised Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) Guidelines. See: https://www.rcpa.edu.au/Library/Practising-Pathology/PTIS/SPIA-Terminology-Reference

Name: spia-haematology-refset
Title: RCPA - SPIA Haematology Value Set
Description: Standard codes for use in reporting haematology pathology results in Australia. Content is derived from the RCPA Standardised Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) Guidelines. See: https://www.rcpa.edu.au/Library/Practising-Pathology/PTIS/SPIA-Terminology-Reference

Name: spia-immunopathology-refset
Title: RCPA - SPIA Immunopathology Value Set
Description: Standard codes for use in reporting immunopathology results in Australia. Content is derived from the RCPA Standardised Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) Guidelines. See: https://www.rcpa.edu.au/Library/Practising-Pathology/PTIS/SPIA-Terminology-Reference

Name: spia-microbiology-serology-molecular-refset
Title: RCPA - SPIA Microbiology Serology Molecular Pathology Value Set
Description: Standard codes for use in reporting microbiology pathology results in Australia. Content is derived from the RCPA Standardised Pathology Informatics in Australia (SPIA) Guidelines. See: https://www.rcpa.edu.au/Library/Practising-Pathology/PTIS/SPIA-Terminology-Reference
